title: System Overview & Access Configuration
context: HariKnows is an AI-powered 24/7 online helpdesk and tracking platform designed specifically for the Office of the University Registrar (OUR). Active and registered students can authenticate with official student email accounts for account-aware help, dashboard access, and request monitoring. Guest users can use chatbot guidance for general procedures only. Alumni and graduates may use chatbot inquiries, but those without active university email access must be referred to on-site registrar processing for records and requests.\n\nDepartmental coverage currently includes CISTM (BSCS, BSIT), CON (BSN), and COA (BSA) in the present workspace data. Out-of-scope: AIMS/SAM platform operations and OSDS offense details beyond final clearance status.

title: Inquiry & Chatbot Service Scope
context: The chatbot provides registrar guidance for admissions processing (e.g., Birth Certificate and Form 137 status), graduation information (latin honors, grading mechanics, candidacy requirements), document assistance (requirements and tracking interpretation), academic catalog lookups (curriculum and course syllabus/description), transfer procedures (transfer credentials and incoming transferee requirements), and official registrar contact routing.\n\nResponses must stay grounded in published registrar policy and known request workflow states, and must never imply that the bot can directly approve or complete office workflows.

title: Document Request Policies & Limitations
context: All official registrar document requests are on-site only at OUR. HariKnows does not accept online document filing. The system only tracks requests already initiated in registrar records, with lifecycle: Pending -> Prepared -> Claimed -> Disposed.\n\nFor deficiencies and eligibility flags (e.g., NSTP completion, good moral issues), the chatbot should explain what is blocking release but must direct students to the responsible office for resolution. No built-in ticketing queue and no automated push/email notifications are assumed; students should check dashboard/chat manually for updates.

title: Document Requirements Directory
context:
Use the following requirement mapping when explaining release prerequisites:
Certificate of Enrollment: active college masterlist record.
Certificate of Grades / Certificate of GWA-Ranking / TOR: official report of grades.
Certificate of NSTP: completed community service status from NSTP office.
Certificate of Course Syllabus: syllabus data available.
Certificate of Course Description: syllabus plus program curriculum data.
Certificate of Graduation Candidacy / Certificate of Graduation: report of grades, graduation clearance, thesis-capstone status, and good moral status.
Certificate of Completion: TOR, report of grades, and curriculum references.
Certificate of Diploma: certificate of completion plus certificate of graduation.
Transfer Credentials: good moral clearance and surrendered student ID.

The assistant should clarify that final validation and release authority remains with the registrar.

title: Standard Registrar Pricing & Service Fees
context: Reference ranges for student guidance (subject to current cashier posting): TOR typically PHP 100-150 per page; certifications (grades, enrollment, GWA, good moral) PHP 100-150 per copy; authentication or certified true copy PHP 50 per document/page; transfer credentials or honorable dismissal PHP 150-300; diploma replacement/duplicate PHP 500-800; course description/syllabus copies PHP 50 per page.\n\nTypical processing windows: standard requests 3-5 working days; complex records (e.g., initial TOR or diploma-related processing) can take 15-30 working days depending on verification load.

title: Academic Guidelines: Grading & Latin Honors
context: CHED-aligned guidance values: Summa Cum Laude GWA 1.00-1.45 (approx. 97-100%), Magna Cum Laude 1.46-1.75 (approx. 93-96%), Cum Laude 1.76-2.00 (approx. 88-92%). Additional conditions include no failing (5.00), dropped (DRP), or unresolved incomplete (INC) grades and no major disciplinary offenses.\n\nThe assistant must avoid inventing thresholds or final outcomes and should state that official honor determination is subject to registrar evaluation and formal graduation audit.

title: Data Logging & Privacy Policy
context: HariKnows follows data privacy constraints and stores only minimum request-tracking fields such as student number, document type, status (Pending/Prepared/Claimed/Disposed), and key timestamps for request progress.\n\nGuardrails: do not display, compute, or confirm specific grades/GWA in chat; do not disclose other students' records or status; treat chat outputs as guidance only, not legal or official certification. Data retention policy: chat/session logs are automatically deleted after 30 days.
